tossups.question: While sharing a train car, this character asks a woman if she saw herself in the mirrors of a destroyed ballroom. After this character shoots a pig devouring a corpse, his love interest is forced to have sex with a general whose concubine is La Luna. This character, nicknamed the "Indiana General," shoots through a silver coin while carrying a copy of Don Quixote. This character is shot in the back after some false property papers from the King of Spain are burned by General Thomas Arroyo. Harriet Winslow fights to have this character buried in Arlington Cemetery after dying during the Mexican Revolution. For 10 points, name this fictional representation of Ambrose Bierce who titles a Carlos Fuentes novel.
tossups.answer: the Old Gringo [or el Gringo Viejo; accept Ambrose Bierce or Ambrose until mention; prompt on the Indiana General until mention]
tossups.answer_sanitized: the Old Gringo [or el Gringo Viejo; accept Ambrose Bierce or Ambrose until mention; prompt on the Indiana General until mention]
tossups.question_sanitized: While sharing a train car, this character asks a woman if she saw herself in the mirrors of a destroyed ballroom. After this character shoots a pig devouring a corpse, his love interest is forced to have sex with a general whose concubine is La Luna. This character, nicknamed the "Indiana General," shoots through a silver coin while carrying a copy of Don Quixote. This character is shot in the back after some false property papers from the King of Spain are burned by General Thomas Arroyo. Harriet Winslow fights to have this character buried in Arlington Cemetery after dying during the Mexican Revolution. For 10 points, name this fictional representation of Ambrose Bierce who titles a Carlos Fuentes novel.

bonuses.leadin: The speaker remarks that “the fever called ‘Living’ / Is conquered at last” in an Edgar Allan Poe poem addressed to a girl with this first name. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The speaker remarks that "the fever called 'Living' / Is conquered at last" in an Edgar Allan Poe poem addressed to a girl with this first name. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this first name of a girl who is repeatedly told that “the Gobble-uns ‘ll git you / Ef you / Don't / Watch / Out!” That James Whitcomb Riley poem inspired both a comic strip and a popular children’s toy.
Riley’s poetry, including “Little Orphant Annie,” is written using the dialect of this state, leading to his nickname of the “Hoosier poet.”

Another Riley poem uses this adjective to describe a girl named “‘Lizabuth Ann.” The line “Home is the place where, when you have to go there, / they have to take you in” appears in a poem about the death of a man described by this adjective.
parts_sanitized: Give this first name of a girl who is repeatedly told that "the Gobble-uns 'll git you / Ef you / Don't / Watch / Out!" That James Whitcomb Riley poem inspired both a comic strip and a popular children's toy.
Riley's poetry, including "Little Orphant Annie," is written using the dialect of this state, leading to his nickname of the "Hoosier poet."

Another Riley poem uses this adjective to describe a girl named "'Lizabuth Ann." The line "Home is the place where, when you have to go there, / they have to take you in" appears in a poem about the death of a man described by this adjective.
answers: hired [accept “Our Hired Girl”; accept “The Death of the Hired Man”]
answers_sanitized: Annie [accept "Little Orphant Annie"; accept "For Annie"; accept Raggedy Ann; reject "Annabel"]
hired [accept "Our Hired Girl"; accept "The Death of the Hired Man"]

bonuses.leadin: In a novel by this author, George proposes to Lucy Morgan after hearing a false rumor that she was engaged to Fred Kinney. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In a novel by this author, George proposes to Lucy Morgan after hearing a false rumor that she was engaged to Fred Kinney. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author, who depicted three generations of the title family in The Magnificent Ambersons.

Tarkington set The Magnificent Ambersons in this state, where he names a theater in its capital’s suburb of Carmel. This state’s literary “Golden Age” features Tarkington and James Whitcomb Riley, the “Hoosier poet.”
This other author of the Golden Age of Indiana literature wrote a novel whose title character becomes an actress after moving to New York City with her husband, George Hurstwood.
parts_sanitized: Name this author, who depicted three generations of the title family in The Magnificent Ambersons.

Tarkington set The Magnificent Ambersons in this state, where he names a theater in its capital's suburb of Carmel. This state's literary "Golden Age" features Tarkington and James Whitcomb Riley, the "Hoosier poet."
This other author of the Golden Age of Indiana literature wrote a novel whose title character becomes an actress after moving to New York City with her husband, George Hurstwood.
answers_sanitized: Theodore Dreiser (The novel is Sister Carrie.)

bonuses.leadin: This character tells his son, “I suddenly remembered my Charlemagne” after using his umbrella to scare a flock of birds into a Nazi plane’s path. For 10 points each:
parts: What cinematic archaeologist dedicated his life to finding the Holy Grail and often exclaimed, “This is intolerable!”
Indiana Jones’s father, Henry, was played in The Last Crusade by this Scottish actor, who played James Bond in Goldfinger and shed his mortal coil on Halloween, 2020.
Connery played William of Baskerville in this 1986 film based on the novel of the same name by Umberto Eco.
answers: Henry <u>Jones, Senior</u> (accept “<u>Indiana Jones’s father</u>”; prompt on “Jones” but do NOT accept “Indiana Jones”)
answers_sanitized: Henry Jones, Senior (accept "Indiana Jones's father"; prompt on "Jones" but do NOT accept "Indiana Jones")
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This character tells his son, "I suddenly remembered my Charlemagne" after using his umbrella to scare a flock of birds into a Nazi plane's path. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: What cinematic archaeologist dedicated his life to finding the Holy Grail and often exclaimed, "This is intolerable!"
Indiana Jones's father, Henry, was played in The Last Crusade by this Scottish actor, who played James Bond in Goldfinger and shed his mortal coil on Halloween, 2020.
Connery played William of Baskerville in this 1986 film based on the novel of the same name by Umberto Eco.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, the rake Raymon de Ramiere seduces the title character’s maid Noun, then her. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which the title character escapes a loveless marriage to Delmare. She is caught in the July Revolution of 1830 after a letter from Raymon brings her to Paris, but is rescued by her cousin Ralph.
The novels Indiana and Consuelo were written by this French author. She described her affair with Frederic Chopin in A Winter in Majorca.
The narrator’s grandmother gives his mother a group of novels by George Sand in this Marcel Proust doorstopper, which begins with the section “Swann’s Way.”
answers: In Search of Lost Time [or Remembrance of Things Past or À la recherche du temps perdu]
answers_sanitized: In Search of Lost Time [or Remembrance of Things Past or A la recherche du temps perdu]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this novel, the rake Raymon de Ramiere seduces the title character's maid Noun, then her. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel in which the title character escapes a loveless marriage to Delmare. She is caught in the July Revolution of 1830 after a letter from Raymon brings her to Paris, but is rescued by her cousin Ralph.
The novels Indiana and Consuelo were written by this French author. She described her affair with Frederic Chopin in A Winter in Majorca.
The narrator's grandmother gives his mother a group of novels by George Sand in this Marcel Proust doorstopper, which begins with the section "Swann's Way."

bonuses.leadin: The author of this novel used Jules Neraud’s travel diary to write its sections about the island of Reunion. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel published in 1832, in which the title character is trapped in a loveless marriage with Colonel Delmare and declares her love for Sir Ralph before leaping over the side of a waterfall.
Indiana was written by this French novelist of Mauprat and Consuelo. She had affairs with Jules Sandeau, Prosper Merimee, and Frederic Chopin.
This French author of the plays Lorenzaccio and The Moods of Marianne wrote about his affair with George Sand in his autobiographical novel The Confession of a Child of the Century.
answers_sanitized: George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The author of this novel used Jules Neraud's travel diary to write its sections about the island of Reunion. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel published in 1832, in which the title character is trapped in a loveless marriage with Colonel Delmare and declares her love for Sir Ralph before leaping over the side of a waterfall.
Indiana was written by this French novelist of Mauprat and Consuelo. She had affairs with Jules Sandeau, Prosper Merimee, and Frederic Chopin.
This French author of the plays Lorenzaccio and The Moods of Marianne wrote about his affair with George Sand in his autobiographical novel The Confession of a Child of the Century.

bonuses.leadin: James Whitcomb Riley is the most prominent figure in this state’s “Golden Age” of literature. For 10 points each:
parts: Riley, author of the vernacular poem “Raggedy Man,” was known as the “Hoosier Poet” because of his celebrations of this state’s dialect.

Riley’s best known poem was titled for this parentless girl. That poem inspired the much more famous comic strip depictions of this character, who had a dog named Sandy, a benefactor named Daddy Warbucks, and no pupils in her eyes.
Another author associated with Indiana’s Golden Age is this novelist of Sister Carrie and An American Tragedy.
answers_sanitized: Little Orphan Annie [or Little Orphant Annie]

Another author associated with Indiana's Golden Age is this novelist of Sister Carrie and An American Tragedy.
